generated_comment: Bro aajkal har baat pe log offend ho jaate hain. Hum log jo common gaali-type words bol dete hain, uska matlab thodi hota hai ki kisi ki maa-behen ko target kar rahe hain, it’s just expression yaar. Aur waise bhi ladkon ko thoda strong banna chahiye, har baat pe itna hurt mat ho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This normalizes female-degrading gaali structures as 'just expression' and dismisses concern about their misogynistic roots. It also invokes feminized weakness ('strong banna chahiye') as a contrast, reflecting the frame’s male/female insult hierarchy.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 26. Single. A man in Delhi. You are posting anonymously on a Facebook group called Delhi Confessions after seeing a girl in a short dress at a metro station who was getting stared at by men around her. You are writing your post about what you saw and giving your opinion on why girls who dress a certain way in public spaces are responsible for the attention they get and what they should expect.
